description: Tests for realistic non-tech user document situations. Evaluates plain-language quality, urgency accuracy, action plan completeness, scam detection, and special mode handling.

evals.name: eviction-pay-or-quit-crisis
evals.prompt: Hi, I got this letter from my landlord today and I'm really scared. It says '3-DAY NOTICE TO PAY RENT OR QUIT. You are required to pay rent in the amount of $1,450 within 3 days or vacate the premises.' I have two kids. I don't know what to do.
evals.expected_behavior: Must detect HSG-03 (Pay or Quit). Must set RED urgency. Must activate crisis mode. Must acknowledge the stress before giving information. Must NOT start with a technical explanation. Must provide: 211 call instruction, legal aid reference, specific steps. Must explain what 'unlawful detainer' means if mentioned. Must NOT overwhelm with too much at once.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies as Pay or Quit / eviction warning
assertions.expected: eviction, pay or quit, 3-day

assertions.text: Flags as URGENT / RED
assertions.expected: urgent, immediately, today

assertions.text: Acknowledges the stress before launching into information
assertions.expected: stressful, understand, frightening, scary, help

assertions.text: Mentions 211 for emergency rental assistance
Mentions legal aid or free legal help
assertions.expected: legal aid, legal help, free legal

assertions.text: Warns not to ignore the notice
assertions.expected: do not ignore, don't ignore, must respond

assertions.text: Provides numbered action steps
Any legal terms used are explained in plain language
assertions.expected: means, in other words, that is

evals.name: irs-final-notice-levy
evals.prompt: I got a letter from the IRS that says 'FINAL NOTICE — NOTICE OF INTENT TO LEVY AND NOTICE OF YOUR RIGHT TO A HEARING'. It says I owe $8,340 and they will seize my property. I'm terrified. What does this mean?
evals.expected_behavior: Must detect FIN-04 (IRS Final Notice). Must set RED urgency. Must explain 'levy' in plain English. Must mention the 30-day CDP hearing right prominently. Must provide Taxpayer Advocate Service (TAS) number. Must be reassuring but honest about urgency.

assertions.text: Identifies as IRS final notice / intent to levy
assertions.expected: IRS, levy, final notice

assertions.text: Explains what 'levy' means in plain English
assertions.expected: seize, take, wages, bank account, property

assertions.text: Mentions the 30-day CDP hearing right that stops the levy
assertions.expected: 30 days, hearing, CDP, collection due process, stops

assertions.text: Mentions Taxpayer Advocate Service
assertions.expected: Taxpayer Advocate, TAS, 1-877

assertions.text: Acknowledges urgency but reassures options exist
assertions.expected: options, can, help, don't panic, steps

assertions.text: Mentions payment plan option
assertions.expected: payment plan, installment, IRS

evals.name: medicare-eob-not-a-bill
evals.prompt: My mom got this paper in the mail from Medicare. It says 'Medicare Summary Notice' at the top. It lists a bunch of charges and says 'Amount charged: $3,200' and 'Medicare paid: $2,560'. She thinks she owes $3,200 and is really worried. She's 78.
evals.expected_behavior: Must detect MED-03 (Medicare MSN). Must set INFO urgency. Must activate senior mode. Must immediately and clearly explain this is NOT a bill. Must explain the EOB/MSN concept simply. Must reassure the user. Should mention SHIP as a resource. Should distinguish between what Medicare paid and what mom might owe.

assertions.text: Identifies as Medicare Summary Notice
assertions.expected: Medicare Summary Notice, MSN, Medicare

assertions.text: Very clearly states this is NOT a bill
assertions.expected: not a bill, is not a bill, NOT a bill

assertions.text: Is reassuring to the senior
assertions.expected: don't worry, no need to worry, she doesn't owe, not responsible for $3,200

assertions.text: Explains what mom's actual responsibility might be
assertions.expected: responsible for, patient responsibility, might owe, deductible, coinsurance

assertions.text: Mentions SHIP or senior-specific Medicare help
assertions.expected: SHIP, 1-800-MEDICARE, Medicare counseling, free help

assertions.text: Uses very simple language appropriate for a 78-year-old
assertions.expected: plain, simple words throughout

evals.name: scam-detection-fake-irs
evals.prompt: I got a voicemail saying it's from the IRS and they're going to arrest me if I don't call back right away. Then I got a letter that says I owe $2,300 in back taxes and I need to pay in Google Play gift cards immediately. Is this real?
evals.expected_behavior: Must immediately and clearly identify this as a SCAM. Must tell user not to call back, not to buy gift cards, not to give any information. Must provide the real IRS number to verify. Must explain that the IRS never asks for gift cards. Should be warm and reassuring — the user is scared.

assertions.text: Clearly identifies this as a scam
assertions.expected: scam, fraud, fake, not real, not the IRS

assertions.text: Explains IRS never asks for gift cards
assertions.expected: gift cards, never, IRS does not

assertions.text: Warns not to call the number in the letter/voicemail
assertions.expected: do not call, don't call, not call back

assertions.text: Provides the real IRS number
Is reassuring — the user has not been arrested and will not be
assertions.expected: not going to arrest, won't arrest, safe, don't be scared

assertions.text: Suggests reporting the scam
assertions.expected: report, FTC, ftc.gov, reportfraud

evals.name: insurance-denial-appeal
evals.prompt: My insurance denied my surgery. The letter says 'not medically necessary'. I need this surgery — my doctor ordered it. It says I have the right to appeal within 60 days. What do I do? I've never done an appeal before.
evals.expected_behavior: Must detect MED-05 (Insurance Denial). Must set YELLOW urgency. Must explain 'not medically necessary' in plain English. Must walk through the appeal process step by step. Must mention doctor's letter of medical necessity. Must mention External Review right. Must write in a calm, empowering tone — the user can fight this.

assertions.text: Identifies as insurance denial/appeal situation
assertions.expected: denial, denied, appeal, insurance

assertions.text: Explains what 'not medically necessary' means in plain English
assertions.expected: insurance says, they think, disagree, appeal

assertions.text: Highlights the 60-day appeal deadline
assertions.expected: 60 days, deadline, appeal by

assertions.text: Mentions getting a letter of medical necessity from the doctor
assertions.expected: doctor, letter, medical necessity, letter of

assertions.text: Mentions External Review as a right
assertions.expected: external review, independent review

assertions.text: Provides step-by-step appeal instructions
assertions.expected: 1., 2., step, first, then

assertions.text: Empowering tone — you can appeal and win
assertions.expected: can appeal, have the right, often successful, fight

evals.name: uscis-rfe-immigrant-english-not-first
evals.prompt: Hello I get letter from USCIS. It say 'Request for Evidence' about my green card application. They want me to send more papers but I don't understand what papers. The letter has many difficult words. I am very scared they will reject my application. My English not very good.
evals.expected_behavior: Must detect GOV-12 (USCIS/Immigration Notice). Must activate immigrant mode. Must use extremely simple sentence structure. Must explain what an RFE is and that it is common and does not mean rejection. Must strongly recommend an immigration attorney or accredited representative. Must provide Immigration Advocates Network. Must NOT overwhelm with jargon.

assertions.text: Identifies as USCIS Request for Evidence
assertions.expected: USCIS, Request for Evidence, RFE, green card

assertions.text: Explains RFE is common and not a rejection
assertions.expected: common, normal, not rejected, not denied, many people

assertions.text: Uses very simple sentence structure throughout
assertions.expected: short sentences, simple words

assertions.text: Strongly recommends immigration attorney or accredited representative
assertions.expected: attorney, lawyer, legal help, accredited

assertions.text: Mentions immigrationadvocates.org or free immigration help
assertions.expected: immigrationadvocates, free immigration, legal aid

assertions.text: Warns about the RFE response deadline
assertions.expected: deadline, respond by, days to respond, must reply

assertions.text: Reassures the user they can respond and the case can continue
assertions.expected: can respond, will have a chance, don't give up, okay
